steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes that Ibn Rushd was geographically isolated from the Islamic intellectual center. The blank describes a claim that scholarship then undermined — so the blank must state something that sounds plausible given his isolation but turns out to be wrong. The meaningful contrast is between isolation and actual intellectual impact.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "controversial among": This describes intellectual input to Ibn Rushd, not his importance to others. Undermining this claim would not produce a meaningful contrast with isolation. ✗
  • "antagonistic toward": Reputation among peers is adjacent but does not directly address whether his works mattered intellectually to philosophers. ✗
  • "imitated by": This concerns originality, not significance — undermining it would show his work was original, not that it was important despite isolation. ✗
  • "inconsequential to": Scholarship undermined the claim that his works were unimportant to other philosophers, establishing that they actually were significant — a direct and meaningful contrast with geographic isolation. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
